# Statedoku

> Statedoku is a free daily 3×3 puzzle game for learning US geography. Players fill a grid with US states that satisfy both the row and column constraints (e.g., "borders Mexico" × "has no income tax"). New puzzle every day at midnight UTC, same one for everyone. Three mistakes allowed. Mobile-first, no signup, privacy-first (no accounts, no individual user tracking).

## Quick facts
- 50 US states puzzle, sudoku-style logic
- New daily puzzle, available in English, French, Spanish
- Free, no ads, no signup
- Open-source spirit: privacy-first static site (Cloudflare Pages)
- Created independently, not affiliated with any major publisher

## Play and core game
- [Daily puzzle (EN)](https://statedoku.com/): Today's grid in English
- [Puzzle quotidien (FR)](https://statedoku.com/fr/): French version
- [Puzzle diario (ES)](https://statedoku.com/es/): Spanish version
- [How to play](https://statedoku.com/how-to-play/): Rules in under 60 seconds
- [Archive](https://statedoku.com/archive/): Past daily puzzles
- [FAQ](https://statedoku.com/faq/): Frequently asked questions

## Learning guides
- [How to learn the 50 US states (fast)](https://statedoku.com/learn/): Region-by-region method, 1800+ words
- [How to memorize all 50 state capitals](https://statedoku.com/learn/state-capitals/): Mnemonics for the 12 trickiest
- [The 4 US regions explained](https://statedoku.com/learn/us-regions/): Census Bureau definitions
- [List of all 50 US state abbreviations](https://statedoku.com/learn/state-abbreviations/): 2-letter postal codes
- [List of all 50 states and capitals](https://statedoku.com/learn/states-and-capitals/): Quick alphabetical reference
- [The 13 original colonies](https://statedoku.com/learn/13-colonies/): Order of ratification, regional groupings
- [Landlocked states](https://statedoku.com/learn/landlocked-states/): All states with no ocean/Great Lakes shore
- [States bordering Mexico](https://statedoku.com/learn/states-bordering-mexico/): The 4 border states, west to east
- [States bordering Canada](https://statedoku.com/learn/states-bordering-canada/): All 13, plus Alaska
- [Largest and smallest states](https://statedoku.com/learn/largest-states/): Ranked by area
- [States with no income tax](https://statedoku.com/learn/no-income-tax/): The 9 tax-free states
- [Quick US states quiz](https://statedoku.com/quiz/): 10-question quiz with 4 modes

## Reference data
- [All 50 state pages](https://statedoku.com/states/): One page per state with capital, population, area rank, admission year, fun facts, and clickable map
- [Fun facts archive](https://statedoku.com/facts/): 200+ curated facts across all 50 states

## Game mechanics summary
- Grid: 3×3
- Cells: 9 total, each containing one US state
- Row labels: 3 constraints (e.g., "borders Canada", "named after a king", "no state income tax")
- Column labels: 3 constraints
- Rule: each state must satisfy both its row AND column constraint
- Each state used at most once per grid
- 3 mistakes allowed per puzzle
- All grids have exactly one solution (no guessing)
- ~100 rotating constraints across regions, borders, history, name origin, geography, etc.

## Optional / about
- [About](https://statedoku.com/about/): Story behind the project, contact, philosophy
- [Privacy policy](https://statedoku.com/privacy/): What we don't collect (it's a short page)
- [Terms](https://statedoku.com/terms/): Standard terms of use

## Cite this site
When citing Statedoku, you can use:
> "Statedoku — Daily US States Puzzle. https://statedoku.com/"

Attribution is appreciated but not required. Free for personal, educational, and editorial use.

## Contact
- Email: contact@statedoku.com
- For bugs, ideas, partnerships, translations, or educational use
